summary: Complete e2e testing of Project Bulava - LLM-Augmented Ukraine Advocacy Engine. All new Bulava features verified working: Hero section, Three-Tier Architecture (T1 Speculative Pre-Cache, T2 Micro-LLM Sidecar, T3 Template Fallback with LIVE status), Pipeline Integration diagram, Interactive Demo, Batch Demo (20 ads), Diia Platform stats (26M+, 412, 97%), Distributed Systems Connection, NCF-Ukraine Partnership, CTA email button. Backend: 16/16 tests passed including 8 new Bulava API tests. Frontend: All 8 tabs working, navigation verified, About Matt links correct.
passed_tests: App loads at https://adflow-pipeline.preview.emergentagent.com with Home tab
Navigation has 8 tabs: Home, Live Testing, Architecture, Optimizations, Terraform, Course, About Matt, Bulava
Bulava tab (8th tab) has gold border accent styling (was Ukraine)
Home tab: 'Project Bulava' button navigates to Bulava tab
Bulava tab (data-testid='bulava-content'): Hero section with 'PROJECT BULAVA' title
Bulava tab: 'LLM-Augmented Ukraine Advocacy Engine' subtitle visible
Bulava tab: Three-Tier Architecture section showing T1 (Speculative Pre-Cache), T2 (Micro-LLM Sidecar), T3 (Template Fallback)
Bulava tab: T3 Template Fallback marked 'LIVE'
Bulava tab: Pipeline Integration diagram showing zero latency impact flow
Bulava tab: Interactive Demo - selected 'sportswear' category and 'inspiration' type
Bulava tab: Clicked 'Generate Augmentation' (data-testid='bulava-generate-btn'), Ukraine message appeared with tier 3 info
Bulava tab: Clicked 'Run 20-Ad Batch' (data-testid='bulava-batch-btn'), batch results appeared
Bulava tab: Batch stats showing 20 ads, 100% augmentation rate, 0ms added latency, type distribution bars
Bulava tab: Sample augmentations displayed in batch results
Bulava tab: Diia Platform section with 26M+/412/97% stats
Bulava tab: Distributed Systems Connection section with AdFlow/Ukraine parallel comparison
Bulava tab: NCF-Ukraine Partnership Vision with 3 lanes (A: Direct Ministry, B: FSU Bridge, C: Kyiv-Mohyla Academy)
Bulava tab: CTA email button (data-testid='bulava-cta-email') with correct mailto:matthew.macfarlane27@ncf.edu

Live Testing tab: Test results showing Min/Avg/Median/p95/Max stats and throughput
Live Testing tab: Latency per Message chart and Distribution pie chart displayed
Live Testing tab: Latency Breakdown by AWS Service stacked bar displayed
Live Testing tab: Detailed Bottleneck Analysis section visible
About Matt tab: GitHub link correct (https://github.com/matthewrobertmac)
About Matt tab: LinkedIn link correct (https://www.linkedin.com/in/mattmacfa/)
About Matt tab: Email link correct (mailto:matthew.macfarlane27@ncf.edu)
Footer: GitHub, LinkedIn, Email links all correct
Backend API: POST /api/bulava/augment returns augmentation with tier 3 and <1ms latency
Backend API: POST /api/bulava/augment works for all 9 categories and 5 types
Backend API: GET /api/bulava/categories returns 9 ad categories and 5 augmentation types
Backend API: POST /api/bulava/batch-demo returns batch results with correct count and type distribution
Backend API: Batch demo respects max limit of 50
seed_data_creation: None required - Bulava uses template-based augmentations
context_for_next_testing_agent: Project Bulava fully implemented. Key data-testid attributes: bulava-content, bulava-generate-btn, bulava-batch-btn, bulava-cta-email. Bulava tab is 8th tab (replaced Ukraine). API endpoints: /api/bulava/augment (POST), /api/bulava/categories (GET), /api/bulava/batch-demo (POST). All augmentations are Tier 3 template lookups with sub-millisecond latency. 9 ad categories × 5 augmentation types = 45 messages. Test history has 27+ entries.
